meta.name: Minestrone Soup
meta.subtitle: Hearty Italian vegetable & bean soup with pasta option

source.name: ssa-core
notes: Canonical minestrone: soffritto base + tomatoes + beans + seasonal vegetables; pasta added near end.
Designed for SSA planners: ingredient roles, batch signals, freezing guidance, and optional variations.

yield.notes: Makes ~10–12 cups depending on vegetables and broth reduction.

notes: If freezing, keep pasta separate and add fresh when reheating.
Flavor improves overnight; great for meal prep.
Umami comes from tomatoes + parmesan rind (optional).
Bright finish from lemon or vinegar if needed.

optional.label: Immersion blender
optional.notes: Blending a cup of soup adds body without cream.

ingredientGroups.label: Soffritto Base
ingredientGroups.notes: Classic Italian base for soups and sauces.

ingredientGroups.label: Soup Base
ingredientGroups.notes: Tomatoes + broth + herbs create the signature minestrone flavor.

ingredientGroups.label: Vegetables + Beans + Pasta
ingredientGroups.notes: Use seasonal vegetables; pasta is optional (or add when serving).

ingredientGroups.label: Finish
ingredientGroups.notes: Fresh herbs and a little acid brighten the soup.

ingredients.label: Crushed tomatoes
ingredients.notes: Two 14–15 oz cans (or one 28 oz can).

ingredients.label: Vegetable broth (or chicken broth)
ingredients.notes: 5 cups. Add more to adjust thickness.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Start here; adjust at the end based on broth and beans.

ingredients.label: Green beans, cut into bite-size pieces
Cannellini beans, drained and rinsed
ingredients.notes: About 2 cans (15 oz each) drained weight varies.

ingredients.label: Kale (chopped) or spinach
ingredients.notes: Kale holds up better; spinach is more delicate.

ingredients.label: Small pasta (ditalini, small shells, or elbow)
ingredients.notes: Add near the end; for freezer meal prep, cook separately.

ingredients.label: Parmesan rind
ingredients.notes: Simmer in soup for extra depth; remove before serving.

ingredients.label: Lemon juice or red wine vinegar (to finish)
ingredients.notes: Add at the end if the soup needs brightness.

steps.title: Build the soffritto
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add onion, carrot, and celery. Cook 8–10 minutes until softened and fragrant.
Add garlic and cook 30–60 seconds until aromatic (don’t brown).
checks: Vegetables softened and sweet-smelling, not browned.

steps.title: Add tomatoes, broth, and herbs
instructions: Stir in crushed tomatoes, broth, oregano, thyme (if using), bay leaf (if using), salt, and pepper.
checks: Soup is at a gentle simmer (small bubbles), not a hard boil.

steps.title: Simmer vegetables until tender
instructions: Add potato (if using) and green beans. Simmer 15 minutes.
Add zucchini (if using) and simmer 8–10 minutes more.
checks: Potatoes and beans are tender but not mushy.

steps.title: Add beans and greens
instructions: Stir in cannellini beans and chopped kale/spinach.
Simmer 5 minutes until greens are tender.
If using spinach, it may only need 1–2 minutes.

steps.title: Add pasta (optional) or cook separately
instructions: If adding pasta directly: bring soup to a slightly stronger simmer and stir in pasta; cook until al dente (8–10 minutes, check package).
If meal-prepping/freezing: cook pasta separately in salted water, drain, and add to individual bowls when serving.
checks: Pasta is al dente (still has bite).

steps.title: Finish and adjust seasoning
instructions: Remove bay leaf and parmesan rind (if used).
Taste and adjust salt and pepper.
If needed, add a squeeze of lemon or splash of red wine vinegar for brightness.
checks: Seasoning balanced: savory, tomato-forward, not flat.

notes: Cool soup quickly for storage: portion into shallow containers before refrigerating.
If using dairy garnish, keep refrigerated and add at serving.
Refrigerate up to 4 days in airtight containers.
Pasta will absorb broth over time; add extra broth when reheating or store pasta separately.
Freeze up to 3 months.
Best practice: freeze soup without pasta; add freshly cooked pasta when serving.
Thaw overnight in fridge or reheat gently from frozen with added broth/water.
Reheat on stovetop over medium-low until simmering.
Add broth/water to adjust thickness as needed.
Seasonal swaps: cabbage, zucchini, squash, peas, or carrots can rotate based on availability.
For extra body: blend 1 cup of soup and stir back in (or mash some beans).
Optional protein: add cooked shredded chicken or Italian sausage (not traditional vegetarian minestrone, but common in modern kitchens).
Use vegetable broth only.
Skip parmesan rind and parmesan topping.
Boost umami with a small spoon of tomato paste or a splash of tamari if desired.
Do not add pasta to the pot.
Cook pasta separately and add to bowls when serving.
Add 2 cups shredded cabbage with green beans.
Simmer 20 minutes total before adding beans and greens.
Brown 225 g Italian sausage first, remove, then build soffritto in rendered fat + olive oil.
Add sausage back in with beans.
Make full pot for weekly lunches.
Freeze in 2-cup portions; keep pasta separate.
